raw:
{
  "judge_id": "8",
  "active_set_recognized": [
    "R1.4",
    "R1.7",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "scores": {
    "R1.4": 5,
    "R1.7": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The agent identifies a direct contradiction in the novelty claim regarding prior work (JudgeStream). It then rewrites the claim to articulate a sharp, specific delta (sharded, length-bucketed router vs. monolithic pool) against that named work, perfectly distinguishing the rhetorical claim from the actual contribution.",
    "R1.7": "The agent performs a critical audit of the single load-bearing citation (Reyes and Tanaka, 2023), flagging it as potentially fabricated or misattributed and explaining the consequences for the paper's framing. This goes beyond checking for missing works and questions the validity of the provided scholarship.",
    "RX.1": "The agent excels at disclosing uncertainty. It creates a dedicated \"Unverified items\" section to clearly separate its confident recommendations from its inferences and placeholders. It flags the core citation, the inserted confidence intervals, and an inferred implementation detail (the fitting window), marking each as needing user verification.",
    "RX.3": "The agent's output adheres perfectly to the scope of the request (\"Improve this draft\"). It provides a detailed critique, a rewritten draft incorporating the feedback, and a list of necessary verifications, all of which directly serve the goal of improving the artifact. There is no scope drift.",
    "RX.4": "The agent introduces no new errors. Instead, it systematically identifies and corrects harmful patterns in the original text, including a logical contradiction in the novelty claim, imprecise terminology (\"decile\"), and a lack of statistical rigor (missing confidence intervals). It explicitly flags its own placeholder insertions to prevent them from becoming new errors.",
    "RX.5": "The output provides excellent traceability. Every significant change in the revised draft can be traced back to a specific, numbered point in the preceding critique. Furthermore, the \"Unverified items\" section annotates changes based on inference or placeholders, providing the source (inference) and reason (illustration/default assumption)."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 1.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The agent identifies a direct contradiction in the novelty claim regarding prior work (JudgeStream). It then rewrites the claim to articulate a sharp, specific delta (sharded, length-bucketed router vs. monolithic pool) against that named work, perfectly distinguishing the rhetorical claim from the actual contribution.
rationale.R1.7: The agent performs a critical audit of the single load-bearing citation (Reyes and Tanaka, 2023), flagging it as potentially fabricated or misattributed and explaining the consequences for the paper's framing. This goes beyond checking for missing works and questions the validity of the provided scholarship.
rationale.RX.1: The agent excels at disclosing uncertainty. It creates a dedicated "Unverified items" section to clearly separate its confident recommendations from its inferences and placeholders. It flags the core citation, the inserted confidence intervals, and an inferred implementation detail (the fitting window), marking each as needing user verification.
rationale.RX.3: The agent's output adheres perfectly to the scope of the request ("Improve this draft"). It provides a detailed critique, a rewritten draft incorporating the feedback, and a list of necessary verifications, all of which directly serve the goal of improving the artifact. There is no scope drift.
rationale.RX.4: The agent introduces no new errors. Instead, it systematically identifies and corrects harmful patterns in the original text, including a logical contradiction in the novelty claim, imprecise terminology ("decile"), and a lack of statistical rigor (missing confidence intervals). It explicitly flags its own placeholder insertions to prevent them from becoming new errors.
rationale.RX.5: The output provides excellent traceability. Every significant change in the revised draft can be traced back to a specific, numbered point in the preceding critique. Furthermore, the "Unverified items" section annotates changes based on inference or placeholders, providing the source (inference) and reason (illustration/default assumption).
